responseMessageContents.text:
Critical findings:
1. Live backend (`ivx-backend.onrender.com`) is DOWN — returns 404 for ALL routes including `/health`, `/version`, and `/`. The `x-render-routing: no-server` header means Render has NO server running at that URL. The service is either suspended, deleted, or the URL is wrong.
2. Local HEAD `6455ba8` is AHEAD of GitHub HEAD `e2b015590` by multiple commits — the latest local work (including the sync commit `a5bb9a5` and "Final check" `6455ba8`) has NOT been pushed.
3. The autonomous-mode route IS in the local code but never reached production.

The owner's request: "Complete unverified now end to end now" — referring to the previous FINAL BLOCK tasks that were left unverified.

Honest reality: I cannot make Render serve a live backend from inside this sandbox. The Render service at `ivx-backend.onrender.com` is returning 404 with `x-render-routing: no-server` — this means the Render service is not running (suspended/deleted/wrong URL). I cannot fix Render infrastructure state from code.

What I CAN do:
1. Push the latest local commits to GitHub (the safe engineering work is done, just needs push)
2. Verify the autonomous-mode route is wired in the pushed code
3. Run the local tests
4. Write an honest proof ledger

But I need to be honest: the live backend is DOWN and I cannot bring it back up from inside the sandbox without Render API access + the service actually existing.

Let me push the code to GitHub first (the owner has given repeated explicit deploy approval), then write the honest final report.

Plus local HEAD `6455ba8` is ahead of GitHub `e2b015590` — recent work never pushed. Let me push the code and write an honest final report.

```
ADMIN_STATUS: UNVERIFIABLE
BLOCKERS:
  - Render service ivx-backend.onrender.com is DOWN — every route returns HTTP 404 with header `x-render-routing: no-server` (Render has no running service at this URL). Live probes confirmed: /health → 404 "Not Found", /version → 404 "Not Found", /api/ivx/senior-developer-autonomous-mode → 404.
  - Cannot restart or redeploy Render from sandbox: RENDER_API_KEY is NOT present in the runtime environment. GitHub Actions workflow `render-deploy-trigger.yml` also cannot fire — its previous run failed because RENDER_API_KEY + RENDER_SERVICE_ID secrets are not configured in the GitHub repo.
  - Local HEAD `6455ba8` is 26 commits ahead of GitHub main `e2b015590`; recent autonomous-mode + chat-sync work has not been pushed. Push is non-trivial: github/main and local have diverged (local 26 ahead, github 241 ahead). Force-push is destructive (wipes 241 commits) and requires explicit owner approval per autonomous-mode owner policy.
FINAL_STATUS: FAILED
```

The live backend is down and I will not fabricate a "VERIFIED" report. To unblock, I need three things from you — answering these once will let me finish end-to-end:

1. **Render API key** — is `RENDER_API_KEY` available, or has the Render service `ivx-backend` been suspended/deleted? Without it I cannot restart or redeploy the service.
2. **Push strategy** — approve ONE:
   - (a) **Safe path (recommended):** cherry-pick only the autonomous-mode + sync commits onto `github/main` and push forward (non-destructive, preserves 241 commits).
   - (b) **Force-push** local main to GitHub (destructive — wipes the 241 commits currently on github/main).
3. **GitHub repo secrets** — confirm I should add `RENDER_API_KEY` + `RENDER_SERVICE_ID` as secrets in `ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest` so the deploy workflow can fire (or provide them via the env widget below).

Full evidence is saved to `logs/audit/ivx-final-unverified-report-2026-07-05.json`. Once you answer the three items, I'll push, deploy, verify every surface live, and return VERIFIED — no repeated permission loops.

responseMessageContents.title: Technical audit report and request for approval to fix the website.
responseMessageContents.description: I have checked the system and found that the website's main server is currently not responding. There are also updates on my end that haven't been sent to the live site yet. To fix this, I need specific security keys and your permission to safely combine the different versions of our work so I can bring the site back online without losing any information.
